summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates host identity on install
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js reads os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.version, and the package name and sends them to a hardcoded Burp Collaborator out-of-band subdomain (716bw4e4k31qif2nc1v658fb62ct0soh.oastify.com) via an HTTP GET to port 80 and a DNS lookup, leaking installer-side host identifiers without consent. The package is a dependency-confusion lure against the private @pluxee-connect scope: version 99.0.1 is set artificially high to win semver resolution against an internal package, and index.js is an empty stub that mirrors only a ConsentsStatus enum from the real package's API surface (with a self-identifying comment '// PoC stub — mirrors the real package's exported API surface'). Whether published as research or as an imitation attack, the effect on installers is identical: machine identity is exfiltrated to a third-party OOB endpoint on every install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
